figures.caption: Figure 7. Performance of BRAVE for survival prediction. a, Distribution of patients across 5 centers. b, Distribution of censoring across 7 survival prediction cohorts. c, C-index distributions from 5,000 bootstrap resamples across the 7 cohorts. Boxes indicate the first and third quartiles, the horizontal line indicates the median, the triangle indicates the mean, and whiskers indicate the standard deviation. * denotes the internal cohort used to train the MIL model. d-f, Univariable analyses. Hazard ratios (HRs) for BRAVE and clinicopathological variables in (d) DFS-Pre in the H5+H8 cohort and (e) OS-Pre in the H5 cohort, both based on pre-operative data. The final row highlighted in blue shows the HR for BRAVE calculated using all patients in the corresponding cohort. Error bars indicate 95% confidence intervals (CIs), and P values were estimated from 1,000 bootstrap resamples. Pos. and Neg. indicate positive and negative, respectively. (f) Kaplan-Meier curves for the internal cohorts (five folds), with P values from the log-rank test. g-h, Multivariable analyses using Cox proportional hazards models28. HRs for all variables in (g) DFS-Pre in the H5+H8 cohort and (h) OS-Pre in the H5 cohort, both based on pre-operative data. Error bars indicate 95% CIs, and P values were estimated from 1,000 bootstrap resamples. Statistical significance for HRs was defined as a 95% CI excluding 1. i, Survival curves adjusted for ER, PR, HER2, and Ki67, estimated using the Breslow estimator.

Figure 8. Ablation study. a, Mean rank (lower is better) and rank distributions of 5 pathology foundation models across all 75 retrospective classification cohorts, shown overall as well as on external and internal cohorts. b, Task-level comparison of mean Macro-AUC across all 30 classification tasks for the 5 pathology foundation models. For tasks evaluated in multiple centers, Macro-AUC values were averaged across centers before plotting. c, Task-level improvement in mean Macro-AUC of BRAVE relative to the baseline model Virchow2 across all 30 classification tasks spanning pre-, intra-, and post-operative stages. For tasks evaluated in multiple centers, Macro-AUC values were averaged across centers before computing the task-level difference. Filled and open circles indicate P ≤ 0.05 and P > 0.05, respectively, from one-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank tests.
